mode_reason: Initial md5 staleness probe found 4-of-5 git-owned probed files diverging from main (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, build-scripts/generate-html.js, docs/index.html). Investigation showed 3 of those (the data files + docs/index.html) were stranded universal-pusher rescue payload from a 09:26 decider 403 — workspace-sync rescued them at 11:38 (mid-run) so they are now FIXED in main. The 4th — build-scripts/generate-html.js — is in NEVER_PUSH and therefore stranded for the second consecutive day, this time carrying EXP-271 (.ds-falsifiability-module rename + WCAG remediation). Same shape as yesterday's ISS-1605, different EXP. Operator action required.

findings.headline: Same drift pattern as yesterday, different EXP. Decider integrated EXP-271 (.ds-falsifiability-module CSS rename + WCAG remediation, 4 patches, 17 new tests passing) at 01:18:21Z; pushed at 01:29:57Z; got 403 on Devilwench/Devilwitch authorship. Universal-pusher rescued the 14 affected data files (wins.json, sections.json, expansion-tracker, decider state, etc.) but build-scripts/generate-html.js is in NEVER_PUSH and therefore stranded in FUSE for the second consecutive day. Workspace-sync at 11:38 picked up the rescued data files; generate-html.js remains the operator-action item.

self_fixes_deferred.reason: Tinker is allowed to edit its own outputs and PROP files. PROP-010 verification has unescaped single quotes inside double quotes around `target === 'sync-workspace'` that confuses bash even after PROP-010 was correctly implemented. The PROP itself is implemented; this is just a self-test bug.

self_fixes_deferred.proposed: grep -Fq "target === 'sync-workspace'" build.js && grep -Fq 'syncToWorkspace()' build.js && grep -Fq 'node build.js sync-workspace' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
self_fixes_deferred.deferred_reason: Wanted to confirm root cause first; will apply next run.

tests_run_reason: Mode 2; decider 09:24Z run already confirmed 3501 passed during EXP-271 integration.
other_side_of_the_argument: Yesterday's report flagged that maybe the structural fix is to STOP fighting the 403 and accept the two-track model. Today's evidence reinforces that view: universal-pusher worked perfectly for the data layer (rescued 14 + 15 + 15 files across three 403s, with workspace-sync rescuing them within an hour each). The system functions exactly as designed for everything except source code, and the source code split is intentional (humans review build-script changes). The real question is not 'how do we fix the 403' but 'how often does the operator want to manually round-trip generate-html.js patches?' If the answer is 'less', then the move is to thicken the analyst/curmudgeon front end so fewer EXPs need source-code patches (e.g., more pure-data EXPs that touch only sections.json), not to hack around the auth boundary. EXP-271 is a fairly mechanical CSS rename + 4-line WCAG fix — it could be expressed as a pure-data sectional patch if generate-html.js exposed those tokens via a config object instead of inline strings. That's a Mode 3 architecture project, not a Mode 2 fix. Note also: this strand is much smaller than yesterday's — 1 EXP not 5 — which suggests the operator's daily clean-clone cadence is keeping up with the integration rate. The system is in steady state, just not zero-friction.

top_recommendations.action: Apply EXP-271 to build-scripts/generate-html.js + test.js via clean clone. Read monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-271-falsifiability-module-remediation.json (4 exact_string_replace patches), apply, run tests/run.sh (should still show 3501 passed + 17 new EXP-271 assertions), commit, push. The EXP is fully integration-tested in the decider's clone — patches are mechanical.

Decide structural posture on decider 403. Three options: (a) accept two-track model (operator manually integrates source-code patches; current state); (b) extend universal-pusher with rescue tags for build-scripts (loses review boundary); (c) chase the 403 root cause (env, IP, identity). Same recommendation as yesterday; no new evidence for any option.

Open-issues sweep. 154 open, 101 older than 7 days, growing ~30/day. Without a sweep, the open-issues.json is becoming a write-only log. Decider's daily-report 'work_done_this_run' shows it processes ~20 proposals/run but creates ~4-5 issues/run too — net positive accumulation.
Apply the PROP-010 verification_pattern fix (use grep -Fq instead of grep -q for the literal-string match). Mechanical, low risk.

Close PROP-005 wontfix (10+ days STILL_BROKEN, drain-mode operating point makes the no-op-rate metric not actionable). Reassess PROP-008/-011/-012 in light of drain-mode operating point — if they're still relevant, demote to next-quarter; if not, close wontfix.
